data.description:
# Claim integration: fast signature check + --force override

## Why
The gate fires at open->in_progress, but the heavy review runs out-of-band; `claim` must stay fast and never block on an LLM. It only verifies the attestation and offers a recorded override.

## What
Wire the signed-attestation check into `claim`, plus the out-of-band review entrypoint.

## Scope
- `claim` performs a fast signature-verify only (no LLM, no network round-trip; target p95 < ~50ms); a missing or material-edit-invalidated signature blocks the claim.
- `--force` override with a recorded justification (structured, audited).
- Name the out-of-band review entrypoint: `rebar review-plan <ticket>` / an MCP `run_workflow` tool, mirroring `rebar verify-completion`; triggered by the agent on a claim-block or by CI.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Claiming an open ticket requires a valid, non-stale signed attestation; absence/invalidation blocks with a clear message; tested.
- [ ] `--force` claims succeed with a recorded, audited justification; tested.
- [ ] The synchronous claim path does no LLM/network work (signature-verify only); a latency test asserts p95 < ~50ms.
- [ ] A documented review entrypoint produces the signature out-of-band.
data.title: Claim integration: fast signature check + --force override
